feedback.correctItalian: Perfetto! Un modo informale per chiedere se qualcuno è giunto a destinazione.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, significa 'Hey! Did you arrive?'.
feedback.correctEnglish: Perfect! An informal way to ask if someone has reached their destination.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, it means 'Hey! Did you arrive?'.

feedback.correctItalian: Ottimo! 'Appena entrata' (o 'appena entrato') indica un'azione conclusa da pochissimo.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, significa 'Yes, just walked in'.
feedback.correctEnglish: Great! 'Appena entrata' (or 'appena entrato') indicates an action completed very recently.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, it means 'Yes, just walked in'.

feedback.correctItalian: Giusto! Un classico imprevisto dei viaggi in Italia.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, significa 'The train was late'.
feedback.correctEnglish: Right! A classic travel mishap in Italy.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, it means 'The train was late'.

feedback.correctItalian: Esatto! Si scrive spesso dopo essere usciti con qualcuno.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, significa 'Thanks for the evening'.
feedback.correctEnglish: Exactly! It's often written after going out with someone.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, it means 'Thanks for the evening'.

english: I had a lot of fun.
choicesEnglish: I had a lot of fun.

feedback.correctItalian: Ottimo! ('divertito' per un uomo, 'divertita' per una donna).
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, significa 'I had a lot of fun'.
feedback.correctEnglish: Great! ('divertito' for a man, 'divertita' for a woman).
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, it means 'I had a lot of fun'.

english: We must do it again soon.
choicesEnglish: We must do it again soon.

feedback.correctItalian: Giusto! Si usa per proporre di uscire di nuovo.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, significa 'We must do it again soon'.
feedback.correctEnglish: Right! Used to suggest hanging out again.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, it means 'We must do it again soon'.

feedback.correctItalian: Esatto! Con l'aggiunta di un'emoji per rendere il messaggio più amichevole.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, significa 'Goodnight'.
feedback.correctEnglish: Exactly! Adding an emoji to make the message friendlier.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, it means 'Goodnight'.

feedback.correctItalian: Ottimo! Una domanda tipica nei gruppi WhatsApp degli studenti.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, significa 'Does anyone have the notes?'.
feedback.correctEnglish: Great! A typical question in students' WhatsApp groups.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, it means 'Does anyone have the notes?'.

choicesEnglish: I'll send you a voice message.

feedback.correctItalian: Giusto! Un'azione molto comune nelle chat.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, significa 'I'll send you the photos'.
feedback.correctEnglish: Right! A very common action in chats.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, it means 'I'll send you the photos'.

feedback.correctItalian: Esatto! Un'espressione enfatica per ringraziare qualcuno di un grande favore.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, significa 'You're saving my life'.
feedback.correctEnglish: Exactly! An emphatic expression to thank someone for a big favor.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, it means 'You're saving my life'.

feedback.correctItalian: Ottimo! Significa che ci si accontenta di quello che c'è.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, significa 'Don't worry, I'll adapt/manage'.
feedback.correctEnglish: Great! It means you're okay with what's available.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, it means 'Don't worry, I'll adapt/manage'.

feedback.correctItalian: Giusto! Per offrire ulteriore aiuto.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, significa 'Do you need anything else?'.
feedback.correctEnglish: Right! To offer further help.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, it means 'Do you need anything else?'.

feedback.correctItalian: Esatto! Per trovarsi facilmente usando il GPS.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, significa 'Send me the location'.
feedback.correctEnglish: Exactly! To find each other easily using GPS.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, it means 'Send me the location'.

feedback.correctItalian: Ottimo! Si dice spesso prima che il telefono si spenga.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, significa 'My battery is dead/low'.
feedback.correctEnglish: Great! Often said before the phone turns off.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, it means 'My battery is dead/low'.

feedback.correctItalian: Giusto! Quando la ricezione telefonica è scarsa.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, significa 'I have no reception here'.
feedback.correctEnglish: Right! When telephone reception is poor.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, it means 'I have no reception here'.

feedback.correctItalian: Esatto! Molto comune per rimandare la conversazione.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, significa 'I will write to you later'.
feedback.correctEnglish: Exactly! Very common to postpone the conversation.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, it means 'I will write to you later'.

feedback.correctItalian: Ottimo! Per chiedere conferma della lettura.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, significa 'Did you see my message?'.
feedback.correctEnglish: Great! To ask for confirmation of reading.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, it means 'Did you see my message?'.

feedback.correctItalian: Giusto! Una giustificazione comune per la risposta tardiva.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, significa 'Sorry, I hadn't seen (it)'.
feedback.correctEnglish: Right! A common excuse for a late reply.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, it means 'Sorry, I hadn't seen (it)'.

english: I'm sending you a voice message.
choicesEnglish: I'm sending you a voice message.

feedback.correctItalian: Esatto! Ormai si preferisce spesso parlare invece di scrivere.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, significa 'I'll send you a voice note'.
feedback.correctEnglish: Exactly! People often prefer talking over writing nowadays.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, it means 'I'll send you a voice note'.

english: Shall we do a video call?
choicesEnglish: Shall we do a video call?
Shall we see each other tomorrow?

feedback.correctItalian: Ottimo! Proporre una chiamata con video.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, significa 'Shall we make a video call?'.
feedback.correctEnglish: Great! Suggesting a call with video.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, it means 'Shall we make a video call?'.

english: I sent it to the wrong group!
choicesEnglish: I sent it to the wrong group!

feedback.correctItalian: Giusto! Un errore classico quando si hanno tante chat aperte.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, significa 'I got the group wrong'.
feedback.correctEnglish: Right! A classic mistake when you have many chats open.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, it means 'I got the group wrong'.

feedback.correctItalian: Esatto! Quando il telefono cambia le parole senza volerlo.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, significa 'Sorry, cursed autocorrect!'.
feedback.correctEnglish: Exactly! When the phone changes words unintentionally.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, it means 'Sorry, cursed autocorrect!'.

feedback.correctItalian: Ottimo! Uno 'squillo' è una chiamata brevissima.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, significa 'I gave you a ring/buzz'.
feedback.correctEnglish: Great! A 'squillo' is a very short call.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, it means 'I gave you a ring/buzz'.

english: Reply to me as soon as you can.
choicesEnglish: Reply to me as soon as you can.

feedback.correctItalian: Giusto! Indica che non c'è fretta immediata, ma è importante.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, significa 'Reply as soon as possible'.
feedback.correctEnglish: Right! Indicates there's no immediate rush, but it's important.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, it means 'Reply as soon as possible'.

feedback.correctItalian: Esatto! Quando serve una risposta immediata.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, significa 'It's urgent'.
feedback.correctEnglish: Exactly! When an immediate response is needed.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, it means 'It's urgent'.

feedback.correctItalian: Ottimo! Si usa quando ci si sta per incontrare.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, significa 'See you in a short while'.
feedback.correctEnglish: Great! Used when you are about to meet.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, it means 'See you in a short while'.

feedback.correctItalian: Giusto! Un classico messaggio di chi è in viaggio.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, significa 'I'm arriving, wait'.
feedback.correctEnglish: Right! A classic message from someone on their way.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, it means 'I'm arriving, wait'.

feedback.correctItalian: Esatto! Per segnalare la propria presenza sul luogo dell'appuntamento.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, significa 'I'm here in front'.
feedback.correctEnglish: Exactly! To signal your presence at the meeting place.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, it means 'I'm here in front'.

feedback.correctItalian: Ottimo! Per farsi trovare sulla mappa.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, significa 'I sent you the location'.
feedback.correctEnglish: Great! To be found on the map.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, it means 'I sent you the location'.

italian: Non riesco ad aprire il file.
choicesItalian: Non riesco ad aprire il file.

feedback.correctItalian: Giusto! Problemi tecnici comuni.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, significa 'I can't open the file'.
feedback.correctEnglish: Right! Common technical problems.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, it means 'I can't open the file'.

english: Go ahead and send it here.
choicesEnglish: Go ahead and send it here.

feedback.correctItalian: Esatto! 'Pure' aggiunge un senso di disponibilità.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, significa 'Feel free to send it here'.
feedback.correctEnglish: Exactly! 'Pure' adds a sense of willingness/availability.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, it means 'Feel free to send it here'.

english: I'll call you in five minutes.
choicesEnglish: I'll call you in five minutes.
I'll call you in an hour.

feedback.correctItalian: Ottimo! Per chiedere un attimo di tempo prima di parlare.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, significa 'I'll call you in five minutes'.
feedback.correctEnglish: Great! To ask for a moment of time before speaking.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, it means 'I'll call you in five minutes'.

feedback.correctItalian: Giusto! Per declinare un invito in modo semplice.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, significa 'Today I can't, sorry'.
feedback.correctEnglish: Right! To decline an invitation simply.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, it means 'Today I can't, sorry'.

feedback.correctItalian: Esatto! Per rimandare il contatto al giorno successivo.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, significa 'We'll hear from each other tomorrow'.
feedback.correctEnglish: Exactly! To postpone contact to the next day.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, it means 'We'll hear from each other tomorrow'.

feedback.correctItalian: Ottimo! Commentare uno 'stato' WhatsApp.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, significa 'I just saw the status'.
feedback.correctEnglish: Great! Commenting on a WhatsApp 'status'.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, it means 'I just saw the status'.

feedback.correctItalian: Giusto! Reazione a qualcosa di divertente.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, significa 'What a laugh!'.
feedback.correctEnglish: Right! Reaction to something funny.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, it means 'What a laugh!'.

english: Can you send me the link?
choicesEnglish: Can you send me the link?
Can you send me the photo?
Can you send me the number?
Can you send me the file?

feedback.correctItalian: Esatto! Richiesta di un URL.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, significa 'Will you send me the link?'.
feedback.correctEnglish: Exactly! Request for a URL.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, it means 'Will you send me the link?'.

english: I added you to the group.
choicesEnglish: I added you to the group.

feedback.correctItalian: Ottimo! Per avvisare qualcuno di una nuova chat di gruppo.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, significa 'I added you to the group'.
feedback.correctEnglish: Great! To notify someone of a new group chat.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, it means 'I added you to the group'.

feedback.correctItalian: Giusto! Per spiegare perché non si trova l'amico.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, significa 'There's too many people here'.
feedback.correctEnglish: Right! To explain why you can't find your friend.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, it means 'There's too many people here'.

feedback.correctItalian: Esatto! Per mostrare cosa si vede sullo schermo.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, significa 'I sent you a screenshot'.
feedback.correctEnglish: Exactly! To show what's seen on the screen.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, it means 'I sent you a screenshot'.

feedback.correctItalian: Ottimo! Si dice durante una chiamata se il segnale è scarso.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, significa 'I can't hear you well'.
feedback.correctEnglish: Great! Said during a call if the signal is poor.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, it means 'I can't hear you well'.

feedback.correctItalian: Giusto! Quando cade la linea o non si può parlare subito.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, significa 'Call me back (you)'.
feedback.correctEnglish: Right! When the line drops or you can't talk immediately.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, it means 'Call me back (you)'.

feedback.correctItalian: Esatto! Per chiedere una piccola pausa.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, significa 'Wait a second'.
feedback.correctEnglish: Exactly! To ask for a short break/pause.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, it means 'Wait a second'.

feedback.correctItalian: Ottimo! Reazione classica alla ricezione di un'informazione.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, significa 'Ah ok, I got it'.
feedback.correctEnglish: Great! Classic reaction to receiving information.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, it means 'Ah ok, I got it'.

english: What do you say about going out?
choicesEnglish: What do you say about going out?
Do you want to stay home?

feedback.correctItalian: Giusto! Un modo per proporre un'uscita.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, significa 'What do you say about going out?'.
feedback.correctEnglish: Right! A way to suggest going out.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, it means 'What do you say about going out?'.

feedback.correctItalian: Esatto! Per indicare dove ci si trova.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, significa 'I'm waiting for you outside'.
feedback.correctEnglish: Exactly! To indicate where you are.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, it means 'I'm waiting for you outside'.

feedback.correctItalian: Ottimo! 'Pronta' se ci si rivolge a una donna.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, significa 'Write to me when you are ready'.
feedback.correctEnglish: Great! 'Pronta' if addressing a woman.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, it means 'Write to me when you are ready'.

english: Are you still at the office?
choicesEnglish: Are you still at the office?

feedback.correctItalian: Giusto! Per sapere se l'amico ha finito di lavorare.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, significa 'Are you still in the office?'.
feedback.correctEnglish: Right! To find out if the friend has finished working.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, it means 'Are you still in the office?'.

english: As soon as I can, I'll call you.
choicesEnglish: As soon as I can, I'll call you.

feedback.correctItalian: Esatto! Promessa di una chiamata futura.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, significa 'As soon as I can I'll call you'.
feedback.correctEnglish: Exactly! Promise of a future call.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, it means 'As soon as I can I'll call you'.

choicesEnglish: Bye, see you in a bit.

feedback.correctItalian: Ottimo! Conclusione classica di una breve conversazione.
feedback.incorrectItalian: No, significa 'Alright, see you later!'.
feedback.correctEnglish: Great! Classic conclusion to a brief conversation.
feedback.incorrectEnglish: No, it means 'Alright, see you later!'.
